commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: relax BioIBMSubmitRequest n_codons schema le=1→le=3

The previous fix updated the frontend to send nCodons (1-3) but the
Pydantic schema still enforced le=1, causing 422 on any n_codons > 1.
The endpoint already handles multi-codon correctly (use_len_ibm fix).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve 2 test failures in resolution agent

- Add email masking pattern to _mask_sensitive_data() so PII in
  stack_trace fields is properly redacted (fixes test_mask_sensitive_data)
- Extend _mask_sensitive_data() to also process relevant_code_snippet
  field (credit card numbers in code context were not being masked)
- Return empty-dict fallback instead of None in _extract_runtime_data()
  when root_cause is None (fixes test_extract_runtime_data_exception)

All 30 existing pytest tests now pass.

- Use deterministic filenames in initGitRepo instead of Date.now() random
- Increase --diff test timeout from 15s to 30s (matches global timeout)

All 771 tests pass with 0 flakes.

commit.message:
refactor: redesign course page and fix theme architecture

- Rewrite course selection grid with flex layout and alternating row colors
- Add weekend toggle switch in course page app bar
- Sync period labels with grid scrolling
- Move themeNotifier to theme.dart to avoid circular imports
- Remove broken section.dart, apply bold title styling across pages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: IBM QPU threshold-based detection — fixes NOT_FOUND on noisy 6-qubit hardware

Root cause: detection used strict equality (max(counts) == target_bits) which fails
on noisy real QPU hardware where gate errors shift the maximum result.

Changes:
- backend/routers/quantum.py: replace strict equality with IBM_DETECT_THRESHOLD=0.05
  (5% ≈ 3.2× uniform for 6 qubits). Fix field name user_email→email in find_one/
  update_one to match insert schema. Return null confidence when no DB record so
  frontend fallback activates correctly.
- frontend/src/components/GroverPOC.jsx: fix prev-out-of-scope ReferenceError
  (prev→ibmResult outside setIbmResult callback). Use backend detection_result/
  confidence when available; threshold fallback from counts otherwise.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Ed25519 trust store implementation

- Add fail-closed mode to TrustStore: new_fail_closed() and
  open_or_fail_closed() constructors ensure all lookups return
  Distrusted when the trust store cannot be loaded (safety-critical)
- Add is_key_trusted() convenience method for common trust checks
- Add add_key_from_hex() for hex-encoded public key import
- Make get_trust_level() and get_public_key() respect fail-closed flag
- Document placeholder default key ([0u8; 32]) as development-only
- Add comprehensive tests: real Ed25519 sign/verify round-trip,
  tampered content rejection, distrusted key rejection, unknown key
  handling, fail-closed behavior, hex key operations
- Apply same changes to service crate trust store for consistency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: capability badges in model selectors + text-based tool call interception (#133)

* feat: intercept text-based tool calls from small models

Small models (1-8B) often emit tool calls as plain JSON text instead of
using the structured tool calling protocol. Detect this pattern, parse
the JSON, resolve the kernel function, invoke it, and re-prompt the
model with the result so it produces a natural-language response.

Handles both non-streaming and streaming paths. Gracefully ignores
non-tool JSON or parse failures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: show capability badges and legend in all model selectors

Add capability badges (💬=Chat 🔧=Tools 👁=Vision 📐=Embed) to the
startup model selector that was previously missing them. Add a legend
to both the startup selector and /model picker so users understand
what each icon means.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(portfolio): add profile info to llms.txt and llms-full.txt headers (#3743)

- [x] Update `getLlmsHeaderLines()` in `apps/portfolio/lib/llms.ts` to
accept an optional profile parameter and generate profile sections
(name, occupation, tagline, technologies, social links, optional about)
- [x] Update `apps/portfolio/app/llms.txt/route.ts` to fetch profile and
pass it to `getLlmsHeaderLines()`
- [x] Update `apps/portfolio/app/llms-full.txt/route.ts` to fetch
profile, convert `about` to markdown, and pass enriched profile to
`getLlmsHeaderLines()`
- [x] Run linter (Biome) and verify no new issues
- [x] Run CodeQL security check - no alerts
- [x] Fix CodeRabbit review: validate social link URLs with `new URL()`
(http/https only)
- [x] Fix CodeRabbit review: use explicit `ProfileForHeader` field
mapping in both routes (no spread)
- [x] Fix CodeRabbit review: add `console.warn` logging for profile
fetch failures

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>llms.txt・llms-full.txtにプロフィール情報を追加</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## 概要
> 
> 現在、`/llms.txt` および `/llms-full.txt`
のヘッダーセクションには、サイト名とポートフォリオの説明文のみが含まれている。Supabase
にプロフィール情報（氏名・職種・自己紹介・使用技術・ソーシャルリンクなど）が蓄積されているにもかかわらず、これらのファイルには反映されていないため、LLM
がサイトオーナーに関する詳細な情報を取得できない。
> 
> ## 現状
> 
> `apps/portfolio/lib/llms.ts` の `getLlmsHeaderLines()` は
`@ykzts/site-config`
から静的な値（`getSiteName()`、`getPortfolioDescription()`）のみを参照しており、出力は以下のとおり。
> 
> ```
> # {siteName}
> 
> > {portfolioDescription}
> 
> このサイトは、ポートフォリオと技術ブログを統合したサービスです。
> ```
> 
> 一方、`apps/portfolio/lib/supabase.ts` には `getProfile()`
が既に実装されており、以下のフィールドを取得できる状態にある。
> 
> - `name` — 氏名
> - `occupation` — 職種
> - `tagline` — キャッチコピー
> - `about` — 自己紹介（Portable Text 形式）
> - `email` — 連絡先メールアドレス
> - `fediverse_creator` — Fediverse ハンドル
> - `social_links` — ソーシャルリンク一覧
> - `technologies` — 使用技術一覧
> 
> ## 対応方針
> 
> `getProfile()` を活用し、上記プロフィール情報を `llms.txt` および `llms-full.txt`
のヘッダーセクションに追加する。
> 
> - `llms.txt`: 氏名・職種・キャッチコピー・使用技術・ソーシャルリンクを簡潔に掲載する
> - `llms-full.txt`: 上記に加え、`about` の本文（Markdown 変換済み）も含める
> 
> ## 対象ファイル
> 
> - `apps/portfolio/lib/llms.ts`
> - `apps/portfolio/app/llms.txt/route.ts`
> - `apps/portfolio/app/llms-full.txt/route.ts`</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> <comment_new><author>@ykzts</author><body>
> @coderabbitai
現在の実装を元にこちらのIssueについての確認を行い、Issue本文の記入及びタイトルの簡潔化、ラベルの追加をお願いいたします。</body></comment_new>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

commit.message:
Fixes web storage, adds usernames (#4)

* feat: US-001 - Add username fields to UserProfile type

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-002 - Add username generation helper and usernames collection logic

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: mark US-002 complete and update progress

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-003 - Generate username on POST /users profile creation

- Import generateUniqueUsername in users route
- POST /users now generates username from email via helper
- Profile creation uses Firestore batch write to atomically create
  user profile and username reservation in 'usernames' collection
- Response includes username and usernameLower fields
- Added tests: username in response, reservation doc creation, batch
  write verification, generateUniqueUsername call args
- Updated existing POST /users tests for batch write pattern

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: mark US-003 complete and update progress

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-004 - Generate username on PUT /users/me create path and lazy migration

- Create path now generates username via generateUniqueUsername and uses
  batch write for atomic profile + username reservation creation
- Update path detects existing users without username field and auto-generates
  one (lazy migration) with atomic batch write
- Added 7 new tests for create-path username generation, lazy migration,
  atomic reservation, and email precedence

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-005 - Add username update with validation to PUT /users/me

- Add validateUsername() helper for format validation (alphanumeric + underscores, 3-30 chars)
- PUT /users/me accepts username field with 400 on invalid format, 409 on taken
- Old username reservation released and new one created atomically via batch write
- Case-only changes (same lowercase) skip uniqueness check
- 8 new validateUsername unit tests, 6 new route integration tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-006 - Update search API to support username prefix search

- Add parallel Firestore query on usernameLower for username prefix search
- Merge and deduplicate results by uid across email and username queries
- Include username field in search response objects
- Enforce combined limit of 10 results
- Add 4 new tests: username prefix, deduplication, lowercase query, limit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: mark US-006 complete and update progress log

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-007 - Auto-create Firestore profile on web login

When onAuthStateChanged fires with an authenticated user, call PUT /users/me
with email and displayName to ensure a Firestore profile exists. The call is
idempotent (existing profiles are not overwritten) and fire-and-forget with
console.error on failure so it doesn't block page rendering.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-008 - Update friend search UI to show usernames

- Add username field to UserSearchResult and FriendWithProfile interfaces
- Update search placeholder to 'Search by username or email'
- Show @username in search results below display name
- Show @username in friend list entries next to display name
- Include username in GET /friends API response

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract storage demo logic into documented helper functions

- Extract demoStorageMaintenance into 5 focused helper functions:
  * printStorageMaintenanceWarning: displays cautionary messages
  * generateStorageDemoKeyPairs: creates test key pairs
  * storeInitialTestMessages: stores initial test messages
  * storeRawMessage: stores single raw message (reusable)
  * displayInitialStorageStats: shows pre-cleanup statistics
  * storeAdditionalMessages: adds messages for cleanup demo
  * performCleanupDemonstration: runs cleanup and displays results
  * displayFinalStorageStats: shows post-cleanup statistics

core: wire TransferJournal into download/upload for automatic resume (#519)

Integrate TransferJournal into DeviceActor transfer paths with four
key fixes for resume reliability:

- Preserve temp files on read failure when journal is active, enabling
  resume from the last flushed offset instead of starting over
- Add ETag validation (file-size check) before resuming, detecting
  server-side changes that would corrupt a resumed transfer
- Use actual file size on disk as resume offset instead of journal's
  committedBytes, which may be stale if interrupted mid-flush
- Check device operationsSupported for GetPartialObject (0x95C1) in
  addition to quirks policy flags for partial resume capability

Add MTPError.etagMismatch with full error messaging chain
(diagnostic, actionable, CLI). Add 10 tests covering ETag validation,
temp file preservation, progress tracking, and edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add motor runaway and power-loss FMEA tests (#103)

Add 40 comprehensive FMEA tests covering critical safety gaps:

- Motor runaway detection and torque clamping (3 tests)
- Runaway mitigation: force ramp to zero, emergency stop (3 tests)
- Current/torque limiting at configured max levels (5 tests)
- Stall detection: overcurrent on zero-speed motor (3 tests)
- Power loss simulation and graceful degradation (3 tests)
- Brownout recovery and safe re-initialization (3 tests)
- Watchdog timeout enforcement and safe state (5 tests)
- Concurrent safety events handling (4 tests)
- Recovery after fault with manual reset (5 tests)
- Torque direction validation and mismatch detection (6 tests)

All tests follow project conventions:
- No unwrap()/expect() — Result-returning tests
- Uses existing SafetyService, SafetyInterlockSystem, TorqueLimit,
  WatchdogTimeoutHandler, and VirtualDevice test infrastructure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add API documentation for safety-critical crates (#102)

Add comprehensive rustdoc documentation to public APIs in:

- engine/src/hid/windows.rs: WindowsHidPort, WindowsHidDevice,
  SupportedDevices, apply/revert RT setup functions
- engine/src/hid/linux.rs: LinuxHidPort, LinuxHidDevice,
  apply/revert RT setup functions
- openracing-firmware-update/src/manager.rs: FirmwareUpdateManager,
  FirmwareImage, FirmwareDevice trait, StagedRolloutConfig
- openracing-firmware-update/src/delta.rs: All public functions with
  Errors sections
- openracing-firmware-update/src/staged_rollout.rs: StagedRolloutManager,
  DeviceRegistry trait
- openracing-firmware-update/src/error.rs: Module and enum docs

Documentation includes:
- One-line summaries for all public items
- # Errors sections for Result-returning functions
- # Safety sections for unsafe code context
- Device lifecycle documentation
- RT-safety guarantees and timing budgets
- Platform behavior differences (Windows MMCSS vs Linux SCHED_FIFO)
- A/B partition model and rollback behavior

commit.message:
refactor: extract main logic into testable functions in file transfer demo

- Create separate functions for transport initialization, friend address resolution, file transfer setup, and callback configuration
- Extract main logic into smaller, focused functions (createTransport, resolveFriendAddress, initiateFileTransfer, setupTransferCallbacks, startFileTransfer, sendFirstChunk, displayUsageNotes)
- Remove function-level comments in favor of self-documenting function names and organization
- Update baseline.json to reflect code changes

Improves code testability and readability by decomposing main() into logical units following single responsibility principle.

commit.message:
quirks: research and implement additional libmtp compat flags (#518)

Add 6 new QuirkFlags from libmtp device-flags.h analysis:
- only7BitFilenames (DEVICE_FLAG_ONLY_7BIT_FILENAMES)
- requireUniqueFilenames (DEVICE_FLAG_UNIQUE_FILENAMES)
- cannotHandleDateModified (DEVICE_FLAG_CANNOT_HANDLE_DATEMODIFIED)
- brokenBatteryLevel (DEVICE_FLAG_BROKEN_BATTERY_LEVEL)
- alwaysProbeDescriptor (DEVICE_FLAG_ALWAYS_PROBE_DESCRIPTOR)
- deleteSendsEvent (DEVICE_FLAG_DELETE_SENDS_EVENT)

Wire only7BitFilenames into PathSanitizer.sanitizeForMTP() and
DeviceActor write path. Full Codable support with backward-compat
defaults. Update MTPCompatibilityResearch.md with expanded coverage
analysis (22 of 32 libmtp flags now covered).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SwiftMTP-dev
repository.description: Swift-native MacOS and iOS MTP library

commit.message:
docs: Pixel 7 bulk timeout deep analysis + quirks update (#517)

Add comprehensive root cause analysis for the Pixel 7 (18d1:4ee1) bulk
transfer timeout issue. Key findings:

- libmtp uses double libusb_reset_device() (FORCE_RESET_ON_CLOSE) while
  SwiftMTP does a single reset — the second reset may be required for
  macOS/Darwin USBDeviceReEnumerate to fully flush IOKit state
- Android FunctionFS-based MTP server needs 70-250ms to reinitialize
  endpoints after USB bus reset
- libmtp uses 60,000ms operational timeout (LONG_TIMEOUT flag) vs
  SwiftMTP's 30,000ms
- libmtp skips set_interface_alt_setting on macOS (#ifndef __APPLE__)

Quirks changes (google-pixel-7-4ee1):
- ioTimeoutMs: 30000 → 60000 (match libmtp LONG_TIMEOUT)
- Add forceDoubleReset flag (match libmtp FORCE_RESET_ON_CLOSE)
- Add longTimeout flag

New file: Docs/pixel7-bulk-timeout-analysis.md
Updated: Specs/quirks.json, SwiftMTPQuirks/Resources/quirks.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SwiftMTP-dev
repository.description: Swift-native MacOS and iOS MTP library

commit.message:
fileprovider: implement thumbnail provision via MTP GetThumb (#516)

Add NSFileProviderThumbnailing support using MTP GetThumb operation (0x100A).

Changes:
- Add ThumbnailRequest/ThumbnailResponse XPC types with NSSecureCoding
- Add getThumbnail method to MTPXPCService protocol and implementation
- Implement fetchThumbnails on MTPFileProviderExtension with in-memory
  cache (LRU-style, 200 entry limit) and graceful error handling
- Add 9 tests covering success, caching, error handling, batch requests,
  invalid identifiers, missing XPC, and NSSecureCoding round-trips
- Update all existing mock XPC services to conform to new protocol method
- Update FileProvider-TechPreview.md capability truth table

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SwiftMTP-dev

commit.message:
fix: make first model in each provider group selectable (#131)

AddChoiceGroup uses its first argument as a non-selectable group header,
which made the first model per provider (e.g. Mistral Large) grayed out
and unselectable. Switch to AddChoices so all models are selectable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix boolector build: wrap cmake for CMake policy version compatibility

btor2tools CMakeLists.txt uses cmake_minimum_required < 3.5 which
newer CMake rejects. Wrap cmake to inject -DCMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM=3.5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add MLX backend infrastructure for Apple Silicon (experimental, not production-ready) (#99)

* Add MLX backend implementation with tests

Co-authored-by: kmesiab <161768+kmesiab@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update documentation and CLI for MLX backend

Co-authored-by: kmesiab <161768+kmesiab@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix test for MLX backend models

Co-authored-by: kmesiab <161768+kmesiab@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback: revert MLX CLI integration, improve platform checks, fix dependencies

- Reverted CLI to only support production-ready backends (ollama, llamacpp)
- Added check_mlx() to dependencies.py with proper platform/architecture validation
- Enhanced MLXBackend.install() to check both platform.system() and platform.machine()
- Fixed BackendManager.recommend_backend() to only recommend available backends
- Updated ModelDownloader to skip MLX models (they use mlx_lm's auto-download)
- Fixed README to reflect actual default backend (llamacpp, not auto)
- Fixed tests to match new behavior
- Added note that MLX is experimental and not yet production-ready

Co-authored-by: kmesiab <161768+kmesiab@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix review feedback: test mocking, messaging, and documentation

- Fixed test_detect_available to use sys.modules patching (not builtins.__import__)
- Added platform.machine() patch to test_install_pip_failure for proper test coverage
- Updated backend recommendation fallback message (removed "will auto-install")
- Fixed check_mlx() install instructions to remove unavailable CLI command
- Changed MLXBackend.configure() to use max_output_tokens instead of context_length
- Updated README to remove unsupported MLX init command and clarify experimental status

Co-authored-by: kmesiab <161768+kmesiab@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI failures: KeyError in recommend_backend and black formatting

- Fixed recommend_backend to use .get() instead of dict access to avoid KeyError when backends are mocked in tests
- Ran black formatter on backend_manager.py, mlx_backend.py, and test_mlx_backend.py
- All 35 backend tests now passing
- Black formatting check now passing

Co-authored-by: kmesiab <161768+kmesiab@users.noreply.github.com>

- Added blank line after imports in mlx_backend.py

- All ruff checks now passing
